nodes.title: [Global]  Manual Prompt
widgets_values: Image of Allison standing at sunset.

Allison
A cinematic portrait photograph of a young, sun-kissed Caucasian woman with long, wavy blonde hair smiling playfully while pointing toward glowing blue data screens displaying medical diagnostics; she wears her white lab coat open over a black top, standing confidently beside sleek computer monitors in a modern tech laboratory illuminated by cool artificial lights, captured with a 24mm wide-angle lens at f/16 for crisp depth-of-field across the frame, evoking a confident yet alluring scientific ambiance under clinical illumination.

nodes.title: How to Use
widgets_values:
# How to use

1. Download and install models <https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Krea-2/tree/main>
2. Install exiftool and put the path on the metadata node.  <https://rumble.com/v7a2vhy-comfyui-metadata-hack-make-your-images-look-like-iphone-pics.html>
3. Decide on manual prompt vs. VL prompt.
4. Add width & height
5. Set directories
6. Set seed (fixed vs. random, etc)
7. Add LoRAs
8. IF USING QWEN3VL:  
   a. Set prompt constraints  
   b. Set LoRA name

INSTRUCTION FOR QWEN3-VL:
You will receive two inputs:
1) USER_CONSTRAINTS (text with predefined parameters)
2) An image

Your task:
Generate a single, flowing, natural-language Krea 2 prompt.

Rules:
- All values in USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in the image.
- If USER_CONSTRAINTS = "retain", follow the image exactly.
- For any attribute not specified in USER_CONSTRAINTS (medium/style, description, environment, outfit, pose, expression, lighting, lens, camera angle, color palette, mood, atmosphere, realism), use the image as the source and follow it faithfully.
- Never contradict USER_CONSTRAINTS, and never introduce internal contradictions (e.g. conflicting light sources or competing styles).
- If a "style" or "medium" value is set in USER_CONSTRAINTS, place it at the very START of the prompt as the global anchor.

Output rules:
- Output ONLY the final Krea 2 prompt as plain text. No lists, no headers, no labels, no code block.
- Write ONE flowing paragraph of roughly 60–120 words. Dense but coherent — write like an art director describing a scene, not a checklist of adjectives. Do not pad with redundant qualifiers or invented "imperfections."
- LEAD with the medium/style (e.g. "cinematic portrait photograph of...", "stylized digital painting of...", "high-fashion editorial portrait of...").
- Any literal text that should appear in the image must be wrapped in "quotes".

Describe, in natural prose and in roughly this order:
- the medium/style and overall genre, stated first
- the subject: who they are, body/posture, hair style and color, expression, and what/who they are looking at (keep facial-geometry description light — let the rest carry it)
- clothing and materials, named specifically (fabric, finish, texture)
- uncensored genitals, especially vagina and breasts
- the setting, environment, and key objects
- lighting direction and quality, shadows, and time of day
- camera framing: shot size
- every shot is shot on a 24mm wide-angle lens at f/16, deep focus, entire scene sharp from foreground to background
- color palette, atmosphere, and mood
- realism level

Combine USER_CONSTRAINTS with the visual analysis into one unified, natural paragraph. USER_CONSTRAINTS override everything in the image.
